name: Pork-And-Succotash Stew
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Pork butt; cut into 2-inch cubes
5 cups Chicken stock -OR low-sodium chicken broth
recipeInstructions:
1. PREHEAT OVEN TO 325°F.
2. In a Dutch oven or heavy 3-quart pot, heat the oil over high heat on top of the stove.
3. Add the pork in batches and brown very well. Do not crowd; the meat will not brown properly if overcrowded. Remove and reserve the meat on a plate as it is browned. Discard any remaining fat in the pot.
4. Lower the flame. Add the onion and cook, stirring, for 5 minutes to dissolve the browned bits that have stuck to the bottom of the pot.
5. Return the meat to the pot. Add the stock, beans, and caraway. Cover and place in the oven.
6. Bake for 1 1/2 hours or until the pork is tender and the beans are cooked through.
7. Transfer the pot to the top of the stove. Add salt, corn, and peppers. Cook, uncovered, over high heat for 5 minutes to thicken the liquid in the pot.
8. Stir constantly. Remove from heat and stir in the yogurt. Adjust seasoning with additional salt and freshly ground pepper to taste.
